body:
Fixes #716

## Summary

- lower command-bearing symbolic program recipes into the write and shell capabilities advertised by the requesting agentic CLI
- keep HTTP execution in the client workspace, while preserving embedded execution for non-HTTP surfaces
- apply follow-up output edits before writing source and preserve Responses conversation history
- cover OpenAI Chat/Responses, Anthropic Messages, Gemini, failure handling, missing capabilities, and the HTTP isolation boundary
- add a real `@link-assistant/agent` E2E gate plus a fully preserved case study and evidence manifest

## Root cause

The agentic planner handled curated recipes but deliberately fell through for ordinary catalog programs. The universal solver rendered the catalog's source, check command, and run command as prose, and no later layer converted that recipe into client tool calls. Explicit agent actions could also execute in a server-private temporary workspace on the HTTP surface. Finally, the screenshot's follow-up verb, “change,” was absent from the existing generic output-replacement vocabulary, and the Responses fallback discarded reconstructed history.

## Reproduction and result

Reproduction:

1. Start `formal-ai serve` in agent mode.
2. Drive it with the real Agent/OpenCode harness.
3. Send `Give me hello world program in Rust`.
4. Send the follow-up: **Change the output message to `Hello 2`.**

Before this PR, Formal AI returned prose and unchanged source while claiming local verification. After this PR, the harness receives `write(main.rs, ...)`, `bash("rustc main.rs -o main")`, and `bash("./main")`; the final response uses the actual tool output, and the follow-up writes source containing `Hello 2`.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` — zero warnings
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `scripts/sync-seed.sh && scripts/sync-seed.sh --check`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose` — 92 integration, 475 library, and 1,574 unit/specification tests passed; 2 intentionally ignored
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `cargo build --release`
- real `@link-assistant/agent` E2E — wrote `main.rs`, compiled it, ran it, observed `Hello, world!`, and completed four API turns

## Evidence

The [issue #716 case study](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-716-c3806a7105d9/docs/case-studies/issue-716/README.md) contains the full requirement audit, timeline, five-part root-cause analysis, alternatives, external primary references, raw issue/PR records, all comment streams, focused test log, authenticated screenshot, real CLI trace, and SHA-256 manifest.

Original failure screenshot:

![OpenCode returning prose without agentic tool execution](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-716-c3806a7105d9/docs/case-studies/issue-716/raw-data/issue-716.png?raw=true)

Real CLI verification: [agent-cli-e2e.log](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-716-c3806a7105d9/docs/case-studies/issue-716/raw-data/agent-cli-e2e.log)

No upstream issue was filed: the Agent/OpenCode harness advertised and executed its tools correctly once Formal AI emitted structured calls, so the defect was local to Formal AI's fallback boundary.
title: Route generated program commands through agentic CLI tools
